title: podman 4.6.2 specify container IP in `compose.yml` not work
body:
### Issue Description

podman version 4.6.2  specify container IP in `compose.yml` not work.
use `docker-compose.exe` on windows to compose to remote ubuntu.

my `compose.yml` like this:
`
version: "3.9"

...

services:
  ms:
    <<: *build
    command: [ ]
    networks:
      network:
        ipv4_address: 18.1.1.2

...

networks:
  network:
    ipam:
      driver: default
      config:
        - subnet:  18.1.1.0/24
`

when start the `ms` service, the IP is a random allocate IP such as `18.1.1.25` instead of  `18.1.1.2`.

windows Docker Compose version v2.2.1

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.6.2
API Version:  4.6.2
Go Version:   go1.18.1
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create `compose.yml` and `Dockerfile` and specify service IP in `compose.yml`
2. remote ubuntu run `sudo podman system service --time=0 tcp:0.0.0.0:2345`
3. use windows Docker Compose to compose: `docker-compose --host "tcp://192.168.1.8:2345" -f compose.yml -p demo up --remove-orphans -d`, replace IP to yours.
4. detect the service that specfy IP is correctly.


### Describe the results you received

The IP of service is not my specfiy IP  in `compose.yml`

### Describe the results you expected

The IP of service is  my specfiy IP  in `compose.yml`

### podman info output

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
We have this exact case as a test in our CI running so I expect this to work. https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/compose/ipam_set_ip/docker-compose.yml

Can you share your full compose file?

`Dockerfile`:
```
FROM nginx:latest

ENTRYPOINT ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
```

`compose.yml`:
```
version: "3.9"

x-base:
  &build
  image: ngx
  build:
    context: .
    dockerfile: Dockerfile
  stop_grace_period: 5m
  stop_signal: SIGTERM

services:
  ms:
    <<: *build
    networks:
      network:
        ipv4_address: 18.1.1.2

networks:
  network:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      driver: default
      config:
        - subnet:  18.1.1.0/24
```

up cmd:`docker-compose --host "tcp://192.168.1.8:2345" -f compose.yml -p demo up --remove-orphans -d`
down cmd:`docker-compose --host "tcp://192.168.1.8:2345" -f compose.yml -p demo down --rmi all --remove-orphans --volumes`

Multiple `up` and `down` to reproduce.
I cannot reproduce, I suggest you update your compose version to something up to date and try again.

My `docker-compose.exe` version is v2.2.1`. I have tried to upgrade to the latest version, but encountered many problems and did not upgrade.

I use podman 3.4.4 no this issue, but has issue [#20213](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20213):

```
WARN[0063] Error validating CNI config file /home/xxx/.config/cni/net.d/dxs_network.conflist: [plugin bridge does not support config version "1.0.0" plugin portmap does not support config version "1.0.0" plugin firewall does not support config version "1.0.0" plugin tuning does not support config version "1.0.0"] 
ERRO[0063] error loading cached network config: network "dxs_network" not found in CNI cache 
WARN[0063] falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk 
WARN[0063] Error validating CNI config file /home/xxx/.config/cni/net.d/dxs_network.conflist: [plugin bridge does not support config version "1.0.0" plugin portmap does not support config version "1.0.0" plugin firewall does not support config version "1.0.0" plugin tuning does not support config version "1.0.0"] 
ERRO[0063] Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container b84152202a30908f7961beac738b7216b88c2ced9e885c2de2cd460d133284e9: CNI network "dxs_network" not found 
```

How to resove it if I keep use ubuntu default podman version 3.4.4? Now I change the `cniVersion` from `1.0.0` to `0.4.0` manually everytime.Very troublesome.

btw, I use docker instead of podman work correctly.

The simple answer is do not use CNI, use netavark. But especially do not use old outdated podman versions such as 3.4.4, I don't know what ubuntu did but the ubuntu 2204 podman package is just completely broken due the CNI version change (https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/libpod/+bug/2024394). There is nothing upstream can do about that.

If you want an update to date podman you can use `podman machine` to create a fedora based WSL distro on windows which should work much better than the outdated ubuntu versions.
